Digital Executive Producer

The parade will start at 5:45pm at the intersection of Vine and Lindsay. The public will be able to park in UTC lots starting at 5:00pm.







The city of Chattanooga will get a chance to celebrate its latest Olympic Gold Medalist, Olivia Reeves, with a parade on September 3.

Reeves is a current student at the University of Tennessee at Chattanooga, where the parade will end.

The parade started at 5:45pm at the intersection of Vine and Lindsay. The public will be able to park in UTC lots starting at 5:00pm.

They invite the community is invited to line the sides of Vine Street and walk up to Chamberlain Field for a presentation from UTC, St. Jude Elementary School, Notre Dame High School, Hamilton County Mayor Weston Wamp, City Councilman Ken Smith, and a Key to the City Presentation by Mayor Tim Kelly.

โChattanoogaโs olympians made us proud with the whole world watching, and our community is excited to honor them,โ said Chattanooga Mayor Tim Kelly. โIt only makes sense to celebrate Oliviaโs gold medal and olympic record with the folks that made her who she is; St. Jude, Notre Dame, UTC, and her family. I invite every Chattanoogan to attend this event.โ

Another celebration will be planned later this year to honor Olympic medalist Rhyne Howard later this year.

Olivia Reeves is back in Chattanooga after her historic gold medal win at the Paris Olympics.

Notre Dame High School grad, and current UTC student, Olivia Reeves ends a 24-year gold medal drought for the United States in weightlifting. Reeves sets new Olympic records in the process.

title: Chattanooga's Olympic Gold Medalist Olivia Reeves honored with parade | Local News | local3news.com
author: Shay Patterson
description: The parade will start at 5:45pm at the intersection of Vine and Lindsay. The public will be able to park in UTC lots starting at 5:00pm.
